title: Binghamia multangularis
intro: Binghamia multangularis, also known as the Queensland bush cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Binghamia multangularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Binghamia multangularis is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various ailments, and it is also used to make jams and preserves, leveraging its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Binghamia multangularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Binghamia multangularis places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Sapindales, and family Sapindaceae.
